title: Museum Match Curve: Inverse Design Against Crowd-Out
strategies: no-match baseline: offer a flat schedule with rate 0 everywhere; every donor gives their own unmatched log-utility optimum and the museum spends nothing
forward-tweaked flat match: pick ONE global match rate applied from the first dollar and binary-search it to spend the whole budget, never distinguishing counterfactual giving from marginal response

inverse threshold-and-rate design: estimate each donor's unmatched optimum, shield a high quantile of it from any match (a no-match tier), then back out via bisection the rate on the tier above that exactly spends the budget, and grid-search the shield quantile for the best total

personalized / multi-tier dual-informed design: use more than two tiers (up to K_MAX) with tier-specific rates set from each tier's shadow price of the budget constraint, further separating unresponsive high-warm-glow givers from price-elastic marginal donors
